variant: give me a morning skincare checklist i can actually follow

request.message: give me a morning skincare checklist i can actually follow

response_body.response: here's your AM checklist. keep it simple: cleanser, active (if tolerated), hydration, SPF. that's it.  the biggest mistake people make is stacking too much. one active at a time, cleanser first so products actually absorb, SPF last because UV worsens everything you're trying to fix. if your skin is reactive or inflamed right now, drop the active entirely and just do cleanser → hydration → moisturizer → SPF until your barrier stabilizes.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%
products.description: 10% niacinamide for sebum control and pore appearance.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

items: apply hydrating toner or mist on damp skin (optional but helps absorption)
apply one active if using AM (azelaic is safe; niacinamide is gentle)
layer hydrating serum (hyaluronic acid, beta-glucan, or peptides)
